$schema: This is descriptive research evidence, not normative doctrine
note: Film case studies with documented color strategies. NO Prisma color assignments. All descriptions use source terminology and are illustrative only.

films.title: The Wizard of Oz
films.described_color_strategy: Narrative Bifurcation: Transition from sepia-toned Kansas to Technicolor Oz symbolizes shift from mundane reality to fantasy. Ruby Slippers changed from book's silver to maximize red saturation against Yellow Brick Road.
films.narrative_function_of_color: Color as reality marker - sepia represents mundane real world, Technicolor represents fantasy and possibility. Color transition as narrative device.
films.technical_approach: Three-strip Technicolor process for Oz sequences, sepia toning for Kansas sequences
films.cultural_context: Golden Age Hollywood spectacle, Technicolor as premium experience

films.title: Gone with the Wind
films.described_color_strategy: Romantic Grandeur: Uses Technicolor's capacity for deep reds and golds to evoke the opulence of the antebellum South, contrasting with the muted, war-torn palette of the latter half.
films.narrative_function_of_color: Color as temporal and social marker - rich colors for antebellum prosperity, muted palette for war and reconstruction
films.technical_approach: Full Technicolor production with emphasis on costume and set design color coordination
films.cultural_context: Hollywood's romanticized vision of American South, Technicolor as prestige production value

films.title: Singin' in the Rain
films.described_color_strategy: Satirical Saturation: Uses Technicolor palette to parody and celebrate artificiality of Hollywood itself, particularly in Broadway Melody sequence with surreal, neon-like hues.
films.narrative_function_of_color: Meta-commentary on Hollywood through color - artificial saturation as both celebration and critique of studio system
films.technical_approach: Technicolor with emphasis on theatrical lighting and costume design
films.cultural_context: Hollywood's self-reflexive period, Technicolor as both subject and medium

films.title: Vertigo
films.described_color_strategy: Psychological Toxicity: Famous for use of 'uncanny green' (via neon signs and clothing) to represent ghostly presence of Madeleine and protagonist's necrophilic obsession.
films.narrative_function_of_color: Green as psychological marker - uncanny, unnatural color representing obsession and death
films.technical_approach: Technicolor with specific attention to green elements in mise-en-scène
films.cultural_context: Hitchcock's psychological thriller period, color as narrative device

films.title: O Brother, Where Art Thou?
films.described_color_strategy: Historical Digital Manipulation: First complete Digital Intermediate. Sought sepia-toned, dust-bowl aesthetic by digitally desaturating greens and pushing toward dried-out golden hue, contradicting physical reality of lush Mississippi locations.
films.narrative_function_of_color: Digital color as period atmosphere - technology used to create impossible historical look
films.technical_approach: Complete Digital Intermediate process, selective color desaturation and manipulation
films.cultural_context: Digital revolution in cinematography, DI as creative tool

films.title: The Aviator
films.described_color_strategy: Historical Film Stock Emulation: Uses DI process to visually replicate history of film color technologies within narrative. Earlier sequences mimic two-strip Technicolor, evolving into three-strip, finally modern aesthetic as Hughes' life progresses.
films.narrative_function_of_color: Medium as biographical marker - film technology evolution parallels character development
films.technical_approach: Digital emulation of historical color processes (2-strip, 3-strip Technicolor)
films.cultural_context: Digital era's relationship with film history, technology as narrative device

films.title: Amélie
films.described_color_strategy: Magic Realism Digital Manipulation: Benchmark for digital color manipulation. Aggressively warm palette dominated by greens, reds, yellows, with blue often removed or strictly controlled. Creates 'storybook' Paris reflecting protagonist's benevolent worldview.
films.narrative_function_of_color: Color as psychological landscape - warm palette reflects character's optimistic perspective
films.technical_approach: Digital color grading with selective color removal and enhancement
films.cultural_context: Digital color grading revolution, French magical realism

films.title: Hero
films.described_color_strategy: Monochromatic Storytelling: Each story segment dominated by single color (red, blue, white, green) representing different emotional and narrative states. Color as structural narrative device.
films.narrative_function_of_color: Color as narrative structure - each hue represents different version of story and emotional truth
films.technical_approach: Systematic production design and costume coordination for monochromatic sequences
films.cultural_context: Fifth Generation Chinese cinema, color as cultural and philosophical language

films.title: In the Mood for Love
films.described_color_strategy: Atmospheric Nostalgia: Warm palette of reds, golds, and amber streetlights contrasted with green corridors creating staleness/decay. Colors function as 'time capsule' mimicking faded photographs and 60s decor chemistry.
films.narrative_function_of_color: Color as temporal and emotional marker - warm tones for repressed passion, cool tones for social constraint
films.technical_approach: Available light cinematography with emphasis on practical sources and atmospheric conditions
films.cultural_context: Hong Kong cinema, urban nostalgia and social constraint themes

films.title: Collateral
films.described_color_strategy: Digital Existentialism: Uses digital video's ability to see in darkness, capturing amber glow of sodium vapor lights and hazy brushstrokes of light pollution. Digital noise replaces film grain as texture.
films.narrative_function_of_color: Digital aesthetic as alienation marker - artificial urban lighting reflects modern disconnection
films.technical_approach: Digital video capture emphasizing low-light capabilities and urban artificial lighting
films.cultural_context: Digital transition era, urban alienation themes

films.title: Black Friday
films.described_color_strategy: Systematic Color Coding: Deep red for aftermath of violence (social wounds), warm yellow for interrogation (tension/sickness), blue for conspiracy (cold detachment). Breaks from glossy Bollywood tradition.
films.narrative_function_of_color: Color as social and political commentary - systematic coding for narrative and thematic states
films.technical_approach: Guerilla-style cinematography with deliberate color coding for different narrative contexts
films.cultural_context: Modern Indian cinema challenging Bollywood conventions, sociopolitical realism

films.title: Spirited Away
films.described_color_strategy: Ecological Color Coding: Red signifies spirit world, danger, and transformation. 'Ghibli greens' represent nature, restoration, and balance. Color transitions mirror emotional narrative shifts.
films.narrative_function_of_color: Color as spiritual and ecological language - systematic coding for environmental and supernatural themes
films.technical_approach: Hand-drawn animation with systematic color palette for thematic purposes

films.title: La Ciénaga
films.described_color_strategy: Tropical Noir: Murky, sweaty, dense palette reflecting swampy moral and social stagnation. Emphasizes tactile and olfactory suggestions of color - heat, rot, dampness.
films.narrative_function_of_color: Color as sensory immersion - palette creates haptic experience reflecting social and moral decay
films.technical_approach: Natural lighting emphasizing humid, oppressive atmospheric conditions

films.title: Devdas
films.described_color_strategy: Operatic Excess: Opulent reds and golds in set design symbolizing crushing weight of tradition and wealth. Lighting shifts to darker tones as protagonist descends into despair. Moonlight symbolizes prohibited love.
films.narrative_function_of_color: Color as emotional and social expression - maximalist saturation for melodramatic intensity
films.technical_approach: Elaborate production design with coordinated color schemes, theatrical lighting

films.title: Pierrot le Fou
films.described_color_strategy: Pop-Art Formalism: Uses primary colors as pop-art elements detached from narrative logic to draw attention to film as object. Color as plastic element like paint on canvas.
films.narrative_function_of_color: Color as formal and medium-conscious element - anti-realistic use for aesthetic and political purposes
films.technical_approach: Eastmancolor with emphasis on primary color elements in costume and set design
films.cultural_context: French New Wave, modernist cinema, anti-realistic aesthetics

films.title: Red Sorghum
films.described_color_strategy: Cultural Color Symbolism: Screen saturated in red to symbolize vitality, violence, and peasantry. Uses vast landscapes of color to explore Chinese history and culture.
films.narrative_function_of_color: Color as cultural and historical language - red representing life force, passion, and social class
films.technical_approach: Landscape-scale color composition with emphasis on natural and costume color coordination
films.cultural_context: Fifth Generation Chinese cinema, cultural exploration through visual language

extraction_notes.methodology: Films selected based on frequent citation in source document for distinctive color strategies
extraction_notes.scope: Represents multiple eras, movements, and cultural contexts
extraction_notes.color_terminology: Uses source document terminology without translation to Prisma color categories
extraction_notes.cultural_preservation: Maintains cultural and historical context of each film's color approach
